metadata.title: RevDeck v0.1 Binary Triage MVP Implementation Plan

include: Project creation/open and SQLite-backed project DB
Native binary triage ingest for deterministic ELF fixtures
SQLite objects/edges object graph with stable ObjectRef
Function Radar with structured score reasons
Three-pane Ratatui workspace shell and command bar
Analysis memory: notes, tags, renames, statuses, TODOs, hypotheses
Findings with evidence links and Markdown/JSON export
scope.mvp_statement: Prove the binary triage loop: import a binary, persist indexed objects, surface high-value functions, navigate evidence, preserve analyst memory, and export reportable findings.

waves.title: Durable Project Foundation
waves.goal: Create the workspace scaffold, SQLite schema, stable ObjectRef model, and deterministic test foundation used by every other feature.

waves.title: Indexing And Object Access
waves.goal: Populate normalized binary triage objects and expose object graph navigation through query and command boundaries.

waves.title: Triage Intelligence And Analyst Data
waves.goal: Add explainable Function Radar scoring and analyst-owned memory/findings/report data that survive re-indexing.

waves.title: Terminal Workspace Integration
waves.goal: Deliver the three-pane Ratatui Binary Triage workspace over the shared core, command, navigation, scoring, memory, and export services.

tasks.title: Project DB, Stable Object Graph, And Test Foundation
tasks.summary: Create the Rust workspace foundation, project lifecycle CLI skeleton, SQLite migrations, stable ObjectRef/object key model, analysis_runs records, and deterministic fixture/test harness.

files.purpose: Workspace definition for RevDeck crates and shared dependency versions.
CLI entrypoint and project lifecycle command skeleton.
Domain types, ObjectRef, stable object key generation, analysis run models, shared errors.
SQLite connection, migrations, repositories, transaction boundaries.
Deterministic fixture manifest with expected hashes and object counts.
Integration test harness for project creation, migration, object ID determinism, and analysis run records.

actions: Create a Rust workspace with crates for revdeck-cli, revdeck-core, revdeck-db, revdeck-index, and revdeck-tui without implementing later feature behavior.
Define ObjectRef as the only cross-layer object identity passed between storage, commands, TUI, notes, findings, and exporters.
Implement deterministic stable object key builders for artifact, section, symbol, function, string, import, xref/edge, score, annotation, and finding references; avoid SQLite row IDs as public IDs.
Add SQLite migrations for project_meta, artifacts, analysis_runs, objects, edges, object_kind-specific tables, score_reasons, annotations, findings, finding_evidence, and schema_migrations.
Persist analysis_runs with adapter/analyzer id, version, input hash, status, started_at, finished_at, diagnostics/error_json, and recoverability.
Make importer/analyzer writes transaction-scoped and separate indexed facts from analyst-owned annotations/findings.
Add CLI skeleton for init/open/import/index/stats/report/tui with structured errors and no broad lab commands.
Create fixture manifest structure for source-controlled binaries, expected SHA-256, expected counts, and fixed timestamp handling for tests.

criteria: `rg "struct ObjectRef|enum ObjectKind" crates/revdeck-core` finds the shared identity model.
`rg "analysis_runs" crates/revdeck-db` finds migration and repository coverage.
`rg "CREATE TABLE objects|CREATE TABLE edges" crates/revdeck-db` confirms SQLite object graph tables exist.
`rg "schema_migrations" crates/revdeck-db` confirms migration tracking exists from v0.1.
`cargo test --workspace` passes with deterministic project create/reopen and stable key tests.

risks: A weak ObjectRef model will fragment navigation, annotations, findings, and export evidence.
Using row IDs as public identities will break re-index, report round trips, and future diff support.
Missing transaction boundaries can leave partially indexed facts without a trustworthy analysis_runs audit trail.

tasks.title: Native Binary Ingest And Deterministic Indexing
tasks.summary: Implement the v0.1 binary ingest path for deterministic ELF fixtures, including artifact registration, sections, symbols, imports, strings, function candidates, xref/call edges, structured diagnostics, and boundary_confidence.

files.purpose: Binary parser, string scanner, import/symbol/function extraction, xref/call edge production.
Artifact and normalized indexed object models.
Importer/analyzer trait contracts, diagnostics, summaries, and run metadata.
Repositories for artifacts, indexed objects, edges, diagnostics, and analysis run updates.
Checked-in deterministic ELF, stripped ELF, sensitive-string/dangerous-import ELF, unsupported, and corrupt fixtures.
End-to-end fixture import and determinism tests.

actions: Register artifacts by canonical source path, stored path policy, SHA-256, size, kind, format, architecture, and import status.
Implement native ELF metadata indexing for sections, symbols, imports, entrypoint metadata, and printable ASCII/UTF-16 strings.
Create function objects from symbol-backed evidence first, then limited heuristic/entrypoint/import-thunk candidates only when evidence exists.
Persist function boundary_confidence/source values such as symbol, entrypoint, import_thunk, heuristic, external_adapter, and unknown.
Build minimal v0.1 edges: CONTAINS, REFERENCES, CALLS_IMPORT, XREF_FROM, DERIVED_FROM; store confidence, source analysis_run, and metadata JSON.
Persist structured diagnostics for unsupported or corrupt artifacts and keep the project reopenable after failed imports.
Make re-importing the same artifact idempotent for indexed facts while preserving analyst-owned annotations and finding evidence from later tasks.
Update CLI import/index/stats paths to exercise the indexing pipeline without requiring TUI.

criteria: `rg "boundary_confidence" crates/revdeck-core crates/revdeck-index crates/revdeck-db` finds model, migration, and indexing writes.
`rg "CALLS_IMPORT|XREF_FROM|REFERENCES|DERIVED_FROM" crates` confirms v0.1 relation kinds exist.
`rg "source-controlled|sha256|expected" fixtures/manifest.json tests` confirms deterministic fixture expectations are explicit.
`cargo test -p revdeck-index` passes against checked-in fixtures without compiling fixtures during the test run.
`revdeck import` on corrupt fixture returns a structured failure and records a failed analysis_run.

risks: Function boundary recovery may be weak on stripped binaries; the UI and score model must expose confidence instead of implying precision.
Fixture binaries built during tests can make counts and addresses non-deterministic across machines.
Overreaching into full CFG/disassembly would delay the Binary Triage MVP and should remain out of scope.

tasks.title: Object Graph Query, Universal Navigation, And Command Pipeline
tasks.summary: Build the shared ObjectRef query service, local object graph traversal, navigation history, and command parser/resolver/executor separation used by TUI, memory, findings, and export workflows.

files.purpose: Object lookup, relation queries, bounded graph traversal, search view models.
Navigation state, history entries, back/forward behavior, broken object handling.
Command AST, parser, resolver, executor, diagnostics.
Query repository methods for ObjectRef, edges, xrefs, search, and relation summaries.
String -> xref -> function -> import navigation and command mutation-safety tests.
Table-driven command parser/resolver/executor tests.

actions: Expose query APIs that accept and return ObjectRef, never TUI row IDs or raw SQLite row IDs.
Implement object lookup, search by display/name/address/string/import, edge expansion, backlinks, and bounded local traversal over SQLite objects/edges.
Implement navigation history that records lens, ObjectRef, cursor/selection context, and broken-link diagnostics for back/forward restoration.
Split command handling into CommandParser::parse, CommandResolver::resolve, and CommandExecutor::execute; executor must only mutate state after successful resolution.
Support MVP commands: find, xrefs, open, back, forward, tag, note, rename, status, finding new/link, export markdown/json, and help for unsupported commands.
Return structured command diagnostics for invalid syntax, unknown command, missing argument, ambiguous target, unresolved target, unsupported-in-v0.1, and broken ObjectRef.
Create seeded project fixtures for command resolver ambiguity cases such as system matching an import and a string.
Keep Graph Lab as local relation lists only; do not implement full graph rendering or source-to-sink analysis.

criteria: `rg "CommandParser|CommandResolver|CommandExecutor" crates/revdeck-core` finds three separate layers.
`rg "navigate_to\(|back\(|forward\(" crates/revdeck-core tests` finds shared navigation APIs and tests.
`rg "Ambiguous|Unresolved|UnsupportedInV01|BrokenObject" crates/revdeck-core` confirms structured diagnostics exist.
`cargo test -p revdeck-core command_` proves invalid and ambiguous commands do not mutate notes, tags, findings, or selection.
Integration tests cover String -> Xref -> Function -> Import -> Back without relying on a TUI renderer.

risks: Coupling parser, resolver, and executor can mutate the wrong object when command targets are ambiguous.
Letting each lens maintain its own selection will split ObjectRef state and break universal navigation.
Building a broad query language in v0.1 would distract from the concrete binary triage command set.

tasks.title: Function Radar Structured Scoring And Triage View Models
tasks.summary: Implement deterministic Function Radar scoring, structured reason records linked to evidence ObjectRefs, boundary_confidence visibility, stable sort/filter behavior, and reusable Overview/Radar/Inspector view models.

files.purpose: Scoring signals, score aggregation, reason model, deterministic ranking, filters.
Overview, Function Radar, and inspector view models independent of Ratatui rendering.
Repositories for scores, score_reasons, function summaries, evidence joins.
Hooks to trigger scoring as an analyzer after indexing completes.
Scoring signal, reason evidence, tie-breaker, and fixture ranking tests.

actions: Create a score reason schema with signal key, contribution/weight, display label, matched evidence ObjectRefs, source analysis_run, and deterministic metadata.

Implement initial signals for dangerous imports, sensitive strings, xref/call counts, function size/span, entrypoint proximity, boundary_confidence/source, and analyst tags as a separate optional signal.
Persist score_reasons as structured rows/JSON rather than formatted text; make every visible non-zero score explainable.
Sort Function Radar rows by score descending, then deterministic tie-breakers such as artifact id, normalized address, and stable object key.
Expose reason-to-object navigation so a user can jump from a score reason to the specific string/import/xref that caused it.
Build Overview and Function Radar view models with counts, top functions, status, reasons, tags/status columns, and degraded indexing warnings.
Show boundary_confidence and source in function summaries and inspector view model so heuristic functions are not presented as exact.
Add scoring analyzer run records and ensure rerunning scoring updates score facts without deleting analyst-owned memory.

criteria: `rg "ScoreReason|reason_code|evidence_refs|contribution" crates` confirms structured score reasons exist.
`rg "boundary_confidence" crates/revdeck-core/src/radar crates/revdeck-core/src/view_models` confirms confidence is visible in scoring/view models.
`rg "dangerous|sensitive|entrypoint|tag" crates/revdeck-core/src/radar` confirms required v0.1 scoring signals exist.
Fixture tests assert score ordering, reason labels, contribution values, and stable tie-breakers.
No Function Radar row with a non-zero score can render from a view model with an empty reason list.

risks: A score without structured evidence cannot be tested, navigated, or defended in findings.
Non-deterministic iteration over maps or SQL results can reorder Radar rows and destabilize tests/reports.
User tag influence must be explicit so analysis memory changes do not silently rewrite objective indexing evidence.

tasks.title: Analysis Memory, Findings Evidence, And Markdown/JSON Export
tasks.summary: Implement analyst-owned annotations, tags, renames, statuses, TODOs, hypotheses, structured findings with evidence links, pre-export validation, and deterministic Markdown/JSON reports that survive re-indexing.

files.purpose: Notes, tags, statuses, renames, TODOs, hypotheses, annotation models and services.
Finding model, evidence links, validation, severity/status ordering.
Canonical JSON export model and Markdown formatter from the same data model.
Repositories for annotations, finding_evidence, re-index preservation, and resolver joins.
Report command wiring for Markdown and JSON output.
Persistence, re-index preservation, rename consistency, and evidence link tests.
JSON round-trip, Markdown golden, missing evidence validation, stable ordering tests.

actions: Implement analyst-owned annotations that attach to ObjectRef and are never replaced by importer/analyzer re-index operations.
Support notes, tags, rename history, status values, TODO, and hypothesis records with timestamps and optional evidence refs.
Make renamed display names resolve consistently in Function Radar, object search, inspector, findings, and export while preserving original stable object identity.
Implement findings with id, title, severity, status, summary/body, tags, timestamps, and ordered evidence links to ObjectRef plus optional annotation context.
Add pre-export validation for missing evidence, unresolved ObjectRef, empty summary/body, unknown severity/status, and draft findings.
Define JSON export as canonical and generate Markdown from the same canonical finding/evidence graph with deterministic ordering and normalized timestamps in tests.
Wire CLI report export for markdown/json and keep export independent of Ratatui state.
Add re-index tests proving notes/tags/renames/status/findings/evidence survive indexing replacement of raw facts.

criteria: `rg "Note|Tag|Rename|Status|Todo|Hypothesis" crates/revdeck-core/src/memory crates/revdeck-db` finds first-class analysis memory models and persistence.
`rg "finding_evidence|EvidenceRef|pre_export" crates` confirms findings are evidence-linked and validated before export.
`rg "Markdown|Json|Export" crates/revdeck-core/src/export crates/revdeck-cli` confirms both required formats are implemented.
Tests prove re-index does not delete or orphan analyst-owned notes, tags, renames, statuses, findings, or evidence links.
JSON export includes stable ObjectRef values and Markdown export includes human-readable evidence context.

risks: If annotations use view-local identifiers, re-indexing or renames will orphan evidence and reports.
Markdown-only export would be readable but too lossy for round-trip and future automation.
Findings without pre-export validation can silently omit evidence and undermine the product deliverable.

tasks.title: Three-Pane Ratatui Workspace Integration
tasks.summary: Deliver the v0.1 TUI workspace: left navigation, main lens, right inspector, bottom command bar, reducer-driven state, local object jumps, Function Radar, analysis memory, findings, export feedback, and terminal-size fallbacks.

files.purpose: Ratatui app state, reducer, event loop, layout, lenses, inspector, command bar, render tests.
open/tui command wiring and non-interactive error handling.
Shared view models consumed by TUI without direct DB access during rendering.
Reducer tests, TestBackend snapshots, viewport fallback tests, command integration smoke tests.
Minimal v0.1 usage notes if documentation is introduced during implementation.

actions: Implement an Elm-style TUI loop: Event -> Action -> reducer update -> view model load -> render; avoid direct SQLite queries inside render functions.
Build a stable three-pane layout with left Workspace navigation, center Main View, right Inspector, and bottom command/status bar.
Limit v0.1 workspace entries to Overview, Binary Map, Function Radar, Functions, Strings, Imports, Notes, and Findings; do not show Trace/Diff/Firmware/Crash/Protocol/Memory Labs as shipped entries.
Render Function Radar with score, function/name/address, size/span, calls/imports, strings, structured reasons, boundary_confidence, tags/status, and deterministic selection.
Render Inspector from ObjectRef with metadata, score reasons, related strings/imports/xrefs, tags, notes, linked findings, backlinks, and broken-link warnings.
Wire command mode to the shared command parser/resolver/executor for find, xrefs, tag, note, rename, status, finding, export, back, and forward.
Add state reducers for lens switching, selection movement, navigate_to, history back/forward, command errors, export success/warnings, and project open/index status.
Add terminal-size fallbacks so small terminals keep command/status visible and do not overlap left, main, inspector, or command regions.

criteria: `rg "ratatui|crossterm" crates/revdeck-tui Cargo.toml` confirms the TUI stack.
`rg "Workspace|Main View|Inspector|Command" crates/revdeck-tui` confirms the three-pane plus command/status structure.
`rg "Overview|Binary Map|Function Radar|Strings|Imports|Notes|Findings" crates/revdeck-tui` confirms v0.1 lenses and excludes future Labs from primary nav.
`rg "TestBackend|reducer" crates/revdeck-tui tests/tui` confirms deterministic TUI state/render coverage.
A fixture project can be opened in TUI, navigate Function Radar -> reason evidence -> note/tag -> finding -> export status without direct render-time DB mutation.

risks: Render-driven DB access will make TUI tests brittle and hide state bugs.
Showing roadmap Labs in v0.1 nav can create false expectations and scope creep.
Command integration must use the shared resolver; otherwise TUI shortcuts and command bar may mutate different state paths.

required_gates: Fixture determinism: import the same binary twice into fresh projects and once into an existing project; stable ObjectRef sets, analysis_runs, Function Radar order, and report output must match.
Re-index preservation: notes, tags, renames, statuses, findings, and finding_evidence remain resolvable after indexed facts are replaced.
Command safety: parser/resolver/executor tests prove invalid, ambiguous, and unsupported commands do not mutate project state.
TUI state: reducer tests and Ratatui TestBackend snapshots cover the three-pane layout, inspector, command errors, and small terminal fallback.
Export: JSON round-trip and Markdown golden tests use the same canonical finding/evidence model.
fixtures: minimal ELF with expected sections, symbols, imports, strings, functions, and object counts
stripped ELF with degraded function boundary_confidence
ELF containing sensitive strings and dangerous imports for Function Radar reasons
seeded project containing notes, tags, renames, statuses, findings, and evidence links

blocking_conditions: Any task planning full Trace/Diff/Firmware/Crash/Protocol/Memory Lab implementation
No Markdown and JSON export path

verification.collision_notes: The repository currently contains only RevDeck.txt plus workflow artifacts. Planned implementation files are future paths and no existing code ownership conflicts were observed.

confidence.reason: The product and test boundaries are clear, but exact function/xref quality depends on chosen parser/disassembly crate behavior against fixtures.
